body.p:
XSLT is the standard solution for XML transformations. There are, however, some exotic alternatives, such as:
UWE test footnote (33/40, callout G): DITA fn maps to UWE footnote/key/desc.

li.p: [[link]]Metamorphosis[[fn:https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/XSL_Transformation#MetaMorphosis*Wikipedia entry on the XML processor Metamorphosis]] is a proprietary language by the company Ovidius GmbH in Berlin. It is mainly used in the area of publishing in the aerospace / defense sector.
There are also novelties, such as a recreation of the XSLT syntax in Erlang: xmerl_xs xmerl_xs

body.p: In this chapter, some selected topics on XML processing with XSLT are presented. The goal is neither completeness nor the best/most elegant solution, but rather the presentation of a use case scenario with a potential approach - the way I would tackle it ...
